heading: Definitions

children.heading: Categorical exclusion
children.label: 1 Categorical exclusion
children.text: The term “categorical exclusion” means a category of actions that a Federal agency has determined normally does not significantly affect the quality of the human environment within the meaning of .section 4332(2)(C) of this title

children.heading: Cooperating agency
children.label: 2 Cooperating agency
children.text: The term “cooperating agency” means any Federal, State, Tribal, or local agency that has been designated as a cooperating agency under .section 4336a(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Council
children.label: 3 Council
children.text: The term “Council” means the Council on Environmental Quality established in subchapter II.

children.heading: Environmental assessment
children.label: 4 Environmental assessment
children.text: The term “environmental assessment” means an environmental assessment prepared under .section 4336(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Environmental document
children.label: 5 Environmental document
children.text: The term “environmental document” means an environmental impact statement, an environmental assessment, or a finding of no significant impact.

children.heading: Environmental impact statement
children.label: 6 Environmental impact statement
children.text: The term “environmental impact statement” means a detailed written statement that is required by .section 4332(2)(C) of this title

children.heading: Finding of no significant impact
children.label: 7 Finding of no significant impact
children.text: The term “finding of no significant impact” means a determination by a Federal agency that a proposed agency action does not require the issuance of an environmental impact statement.

children.heading: Participating Federal agency
children.label: 8 Participating Federal agency
children.text: The term “participating Federal agency” means a Federal agency participating in an environmental review or authorization of an action.

children.label: A
children.text: the agency that proposed such action; or

children.label: B
children.text: if there are 2 or more involved Federal agencies with respect to such action, the agency designated under .section 4336a(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “major Federal action” means an action that the agency carrying out such action determines is subject to substantial Federal control and responsibility.

children.label: I
children.text: with no or minimal Federal funding; or

children.label: II
children.text: with no or minimal Federal involvement where a Federal agency cannot control the outcome of the project;

children.label: ii
children.text: funding assistance solely in the form of general revenue sharing funds which do not provide Federal agency compliance or enforcement responsibility over the subsequent use of such funds;

children.label: iii
children.text: loans, loan guarantees, or other forms of financial assistance where a Federal agency does not exercise sufficient control and responsibility over the subsequent use of such financial assistance or the effect of the action;

children.label: iv
children.text: business loan guarantees provided by the Small Business Administration pursuant to section 7(a) or (b) and of the Small Business Act ( U.S.C. 636(a)), or title V of the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 ( et seq.);1215 U.S.C. 695So in original. The word “and” probably should not appear.1So in original. Probably should refer to , (b).215 U.S.C. 636(a)

children.label: v
children.text: bringing judicial or administrative civil or criminal enforcement actions;

children.label: vi
children.text: extraterritorial activities or decisions, which means agency activities or decisions with effects located entirely outside of the jurisdiction of the United States; or

children.label: vii
children.text: activities or decisions that are non-discretionary and made in accordance with the agency’s statutory authority.

children.heading: Programmatic environmental document
children.label: 11 Programmatic environmental document
children.text: The term “programmatic environmental document” means an environmental impact statement or environmental assessment analyzing all or some of the environmental effects of a policy, program, plan, or group of related actions.

children.heading: Proposal
children.label: 12 Proposal
children.text: The term “proposal” means a proposed action at a stage when an agency has a goal, is actively preparing to make a decision on one or more alternative means of accomplishing that goal, and can meaningfully evaluate its effects.

children.heading: Special expertise
children.label: 13 Special expertise
children.text: The term “special expertise” means statutory responsibility, agency mission, or related program experience.
